zh.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Waltham於1380年出生在英國英格蘭肯特沃爾坦。她於1400年與Sir Henry Horne, Sheriff of Kent在英國肯特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1448年7月17日在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾葛拉芙頓 雷吉享年68歲。

nameMeanings.name: Waltham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Waltham (Lincs, Essex, Leics) or any of the numerous other places so named.

variants.name: Whatham
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Waltham  .

variants.name: Wattam
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Waltham  .

variants.name: Whattam
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Waltham  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Of uncertain origin. If not simply an invention, it may have been devised as a feminine equivalent of   Alan   (of which it is an anagram), or a shortened form of   Alana .  It seems to have been first used by the American film actress  Lana Turner  (  1920–1995 ), whose original name was  Julia .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Waltham was born in 1380, in Waltham, Kent, England, United Kingdom. She married Sir Henry Horne, Sheriff of Kent in 1400, in Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died on 17 July 1448, in Grafton Regis, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 68.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Waltham(은)는 1380년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 월섬에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1400년 영국 켄트에서 Sir Henry Horne, Sheriff of Kent(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1448년 7월 17일 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 그라프턴 레지스에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.
